dung_beetle_landscape

Chloe Cho

2026-05-26

Read in data frame - landscape data pulled from USDA CDL at 1000m radius around each trapping point. Convert data types.

All landscape data is pulled from the USDA CDL. Agriculture includes all annual and perennial crops. Woodland includes all forested habitat + shrubland. Semi-natural habitat includes pastures and grasslands.

dung_beetle <- read.delim("Data/diversity_landscape.csv", sep = ",")

dung_beetle <- dung_beetle %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date, format = "%Y-%m-%d")) %>%
  mutate(farm = as.factor(farm)) %>%
  mutate(season = as.factor(season))

Divide data by year.

dung_beetle_22 <- dung_beetle %>% filter(year == '2022')
dung_beetle_24 <- dung_beetle %>% filter(year == '2024')

Total abundance

All models include Julian date, woodland proportion (forests, shrublands), agriculture proportion (all agriculture types), and semi-natural habitat proportion as fixed effects and farm as a random effect. For each response variable, there is a model for each year.

# Check distributions. 
plot(fitdist(dung_beetle_22$total_abundance,"nbinom"))

plot(fitdist(dung_beetle_24$total_abundance,"nbinom"))

2022

# Model total abundance vs. day of year, prop woodland, prop ag, prop semi-natural (and all two-way interactions). Farm as random variable. 

model1a <- glmer.nb(total_abundance ~ (scale(julian_day) +
                      scale(woodland_prop) +
                      scale(ag_prop) +
                      scale(semi_nat_prop))^2 +
                      (1 | farm), 
                    data = dung_beetle_22, 
                    na.action='na.fail')

# Full model - AIC 1474.4
# Only Julian day and landscape interactions were significant, no landscape-landscape interactions.

# Model keeping the significant effects of the global model. 
# Simplified model - AIC 1472.2

model1a <- glmer.nb(total_abundance ~ scale(julian_day) + 
                      scale(woodland_prop) + 
                      scale(ag_prop) + 
                      scale(semi_nat_prop) + 
                      scale(julian_day)*scale(woodland_prop) + 
                      scale(julian_day)*scale(ag_prop) + 
                      scale(julian_day)*scale(semi_nat_prop) + 
                      (1 | farm), 
                    data = dung_beetle_22)

summary(model1a)
## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]
##  Family: Negative Binomial(0.8049)  ( log )
## Formula: total_abundance ~ scale(julian_day) + scale(woodland_prop) +  
##     scale(ag_prop) + scale(semi_nat_prop) + scale(julian_day) *  
##     scale(woodland_prop) + scale(julian_day) * scale(ag_prop) +  
##     scale(julian_day) * scale(semi_nat_prop) + (1 | farm)
##    Data: dung_beetle_22
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##   1472.2   1505.8   -726.1   1452.2      202 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -0.8845 -0.6816 -0.3486  0.2970  4.9906 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  farm   (Intercept) 0.2218   0.4709  
## Number of obs: 212, groups:  farm, 10
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                         Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
## (Intercept)                             2.306142   0.170111  13.557  < 2e-16
## scale(julian_day)                      -0.861636   0.091835  -9.382  < 2e-16
## scale(woodland_prop)                    0.319427   0.401349   0.796 0.426100
## scale(ag_prop)                          0.192739   0.369980   0.521 0.602405
## scale(semi_nat_prop)                    0.002987   0.236372   0.013 0.989918
## scale(julian_day):scale(woodland_prop)  0.632917   0.189031   3.348 0.000813
## scale(julian_day):scale(ag_prop)        0.362451   0.169705   2.136 0.032699
## scale(julian_day):scale(semi_nat_prop)  0.200566   0.117635   1.705 0.088196
##                                           
## (Intercept)                            ***
## scale(julian_day)                      ***
## scale(woodland_prop)                      
## scale(ag_prop)                            
## scale(semi_nat_prop)                      
## scale(julian_day):scale(woodland_prop) ***
## scale(julian_day):scale(ag_prop)       *  
## scale(julian_day):scale(semi_nat_prop) .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##                     (Intr) scl(j_) scl(w_) scl(g_) sc(__) scl(jln_dy):scl(w_)
## scl(jln_dy)          0.039                                                   
## scl(wdlnd_)         -0.025 -0.036                                            
## scal(g_prp)         -0.021 -0.027   0.888                                    
## scl(sm_nt_)         -0.009 -0.017   0.704   0.631                            
## scl(jln_dy):scl(w_) -0.038  0.126   0.113   0.108   0.085                    
## scl(jln_dy):scl(g_) -0.029  0.009   0.112   0.115   0.088  0.860             
## scl(_):(__)         -0.019  0.024   0.082   0.082   0.058  0.650             
##                     scl(jln_dy):scl(g_)
## scl(jln_dy)                            
## scl(wdlnd_)                            
## scal(g_prp)                            
## scl(sm_nt_)                            
## scl(jln_dy):scl(w_)                    
## scl(jln_dy):scl(g_)                    
## scl(_):(__)          0.552
# check_model(model1a)

2024

# Model total abundance vs. year, day of year, prop woodland, prop ag, prop semi-natural (and all two-way interactions). Farm as random variable. 

model1b <- glmer.nb(total_abundance ~ (scale(julian_day) +
                      scale(woodland_prop) +
                      scale(ag_prop) +
                      scale(semi_nat_prop))^2 +
                      (1 | farm), 
                    data = dung_beetle_24, 
                    na.action='na.fail')
## Warning in checkConv(attr(opt, "derivs"), opt$par, ctrl = control$checkConv, :
## Model failed to converge with max|grad| = 0.00518318 (tol = 0.002, component 1)
# Full model - AIC 1317.1
# Significant interactions - Julian day * prop_ag, woodland_prop*ag_prop.

# Model keeping the significant effects of the global model. 
# Simplified model - AIC 1319.9

model1b <- glmer.nb(total_abundance ~ scale(julian_day) + 
                      scale(woodland_prop) + 
                      scale(ag_prop) + 
                      scale(semi_nat_prop) + 
                      scale(julian_day)*scale(ag_prop) + 
                      scale(woodland_prop)*scale(ag_prop) +
                      (1 | farm), 
                    data = dung_beetle_24)

summary(model1b)
## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]
##  Family: Negative Binomial(1.0222)  ( log )
## Formula: total_abundance ~ scale(julian_day) + scale(woodland_prop) +  
##     scale(ag_prop) + scale(semi_nat_prop) + scale(julian_day) *  
##     scale(ag_prop) + scale(woodland_prop) * scale(ag_prop) +      (1 | farm)
##    Data: dung_beetle_24
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##   1319.9   1347.8   -651.0   1301.9      154 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -0.9794 -0.7290 -0.3713  0.3756  4.1917 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  farm   (Intercept) 0.3095   0.5564  
## Number of obs: 163, groups:  farm, 18
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                     Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                          2.72523    0.20089  13.566   <2e-16 ***
## scale(julian_day)                   -0.82311    0.09130  -9.015   <2e-16 ***
## scale(woodland_prop)                 0.53419    0.25809   2.070   0.0385 *  
## scale(ag_prop)                      -0.30625    0.27210  -1.126   0.2604    
## scale(semi_nat_prop)                 0.18705    0.21558   0.868   0.3856    
## scale(julian_day):scale(ag_prop)    -0.14188    0.09291  -1.527   0.1267    
## scale(woodland_prop):scale(ag_prop) -0.18199    0.21265  -0.856   0.3921    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) scl(j_) scl(w_) scl(g_) sc(__) scl(j_):(_)
## scl(jln_dy) 0.123                                            
## scl(wdlnd_) 0.104  0.045                                     
## scal(g_prp) 0.374  0.107   0.664                             
## scl(sm_nt_) 0.188  0.043   0.651   0.540                     
## scl(j_):(_) 0.124  0.064   0.079   0.143   0.032             
## scl(w_):(_) 0.631  0.122   0.191   0.604   0.329  0.144
# check_model(model1b)

Richness

plot(fitdist(dung_beetle_22$q_0_richness,"pois"))

plot(fitdist(dung_beetle_24$q_0_richness,"pois"))

2022

# Model richness vs. year, day of year, prop woodland, prop ag, prop semi-natural (and all two-way interactions). Farm as random variable. 

model2a <- glmer(q_0_richness ~ (scale(julian_day) +
                      scale(woodland_prop) +
                      scale(ag_prop) +
                      scale(semi_nat_prop))^2 +
                      (1 | farm), 
                    data = dung_beetle_22, family='poisson',
                    na.action='na.fail')

# Full model - AIC 725.4
# No significant interactions. Only Julian day is significant.

# Model keeping the significant effects of the global model. 
# Simplified model - AIC 718.5

model2a <- glmer(q_0_richness ~ scale(julian_day) + 
                      scale(woodland_prop) + 
                      scale(ag_prop) + 
                      scale(semi_nat_prop) +
                      (1 | farm), 
                    data = dung_beetle_22, family='poisson',
                    na.action='na.fail')

summary(model2a)
## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]
##  Family: poisson  ( log )
## Formula: 
## q_0_richness ~ scale(julian_day) + scale(woodland_prop) + scale(ag_prop) +  
##     scale(semi_nat_prop) + (1 | farm)
##    Data: dung_beetle_22
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##    720.5    740.6   -354.2    708.5      206 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -1.72522 -0.66899 -0.03492  0.49259  2.43607 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  farm   (Intercept) 0.05641  0.2375  
## Number of obs: 212, groups:  farm, 10
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                      Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)           0.72550    0.08970   8.088 6.06e-16 ***

## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) scl(j_) scl(w_) scl(g_)

# check_model(model2)

2024

# Model richness vs. year, day of year, prop woodland, prop ag, prop semi-natural (and all two-way interactions). Farm as random variable. 

model2b <- glmer(q_0_richness ~ (scale(julian_day) +
                      scale(woodland_prop) +
                      scale(ag_prop) +
                      scale(semi_nat_prop))^2 +
                      (1 | farm), 
                    data = dung_beetle_24, family='poisson',
                    na.action='na.fail')

# Full model - AIC 632.9
# No significant interactions. Only Julian day is significant.

# Model keeping the significant interactions of the global model. 
# Simplified model - AIC 630.3

model2b <- glmer(q_0_richness ~ scale(julian_day) + 
                      scale(woodland_prop) + 
                      scale(ag_prop) + 
                      scale(semi_nat_prop) +
                      (1 | farm), 
                    data = dung_beetle_24, family='poisson',
                    na.action='na.fail')

summary(model2b)
## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]

## Formula: 
## q_0_richness ~ scale(julian_day) + scale(woodland_prop) + scale(ag_prop) +  
##     scale(semi_nat_prop) + (1 | farm)

##    630.3    648.9   -309.1    618.3      157

## -1.49334 -0.64447 -0.08749  0.59353  2.30818

## Number of obs: 163, groups:  farm, 18

## scale(julian_day)    -0.17285    0.04409  -3.921 8.83e-05 ***
## scale(woodland_prop)  0.21881    0.10880   2.011   0.0443 *

## scl(sm_nt_) -0.022  0.008   0.645   0.462
# check_model(model2)

Shannon

# Look at log transformation.

dung_beetle_q1 <- dung_beetle %>% drop_na(q_1_shannon)
dung_beetle_q1$shannon_log <- log(dung_beetle_q1$q_1_shannon)

dung_beetle_q1_22 <- dung_beetle_q1 %>% filter(year == '2022')
dung_beetle_q1_24 <- dung_beetle_q1 %>% filter(year == '2024')

plot(fitdist(dung_beetle_q1$shannon_log,"norm"))

2022

# Model Shannon index vs. day of year, prop woodland, prop ag, prop semi-natural (and all two-way interactions). Farm as random variable. 

# Zero inflated model.

model3a <- glmmTMB(shannon_log ~ (scale(julian_day) + 
                                   scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                   scale(ag_prop) + 
                                   scale(semi_nat_prop))^2 + 
                    (1 | farm), ziformula=~1, data=dung_beetle_q1_22)

# Full model - AIC 219.1
# Significant interactions - Julian*woodland, Julian*semi-natural

model3a <- glmmTMB(shannon_log ~ scale(julian_day) + 
                                   scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                   scale(ag_prop) + 
                                   scale(semi_nat_prop) + 
                     scale(julian_day)*scale(woodland_prop) + 
                     scale(julian_day)*scale(semi_nat_prop) +
                    (1 | farm), ziformula=~1, data=dung_beetle_q1_22)

# Model keeping the significant interactions of the global model. 
# Simplified model - AIC 216.7
  
summary(model3a)
##  Family: gaussian  ( identity )
## Formula:          
## shannon_log ~ scale(julian_day) + scale(woodland_prop) + scale(ag_prop) +  
##     scale(semi_nat_prop) + scale(julian_day) * scale(woodland_prop) +  
##     scale(julian_day) * scale(semi_nat_prop) + (1 | farm)
## Zero inflation:               ~1
## Data: dung_beetle_q1_22
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##    216.7    249.0    -98.4    196.7      176 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  farm     (Intercept) 0.01411  0.1188  
##  Residual             0.16003  0.4000  
## Number of obs: 186, groups:  farm, 10
## 
## Dispersion estimate for gaussian family (sigma^2): 0.16 
## 
## Conditional model:
##                                        Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                             0.54112    0.04779  11.323  < 2e-16 ***
## scale(julian_day)                       0.01189    0.02987   0.398  0.69046    
## scale(woodland_prop)                    0.10960    0.11168   0.981  0.32641    
## scale(ag_prop)                          0.07255    0.10269   0.706  0.47989    
## scale(semi_nat_prop)                   -0.05028    0.06651  -0.756  0.44971

## scale(julian_day):scale(semi_nat_prop)  0.03578    0.03153   1.135  0.25656

## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

##             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
## (Intercept)   -22.85   10499.45  -0.002    0.998
# check_model(model3)

2024

# Model Shannon index vs. day of year, prop woodland, prop ag, prop semi-natural (and all two-way interactions). Farm as random variable. 

# Zero inflated model.

model3b <- glmmTMB(shannon_log ~ (scale(julian_day) + 
                                   scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                   scale(ag_prop) + 
                                   scale(semi_nat_prop))^2 + 
                    (1 | farm), ziformula=~1, data=dung_beetle_q1_24)

# Full model - AIC 215.0
# No significant interactions 

model3b <- glmmTMB(shannon_log ~ scale(julian_day) + 
                                   scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                   scale(ag_prop) + 
                                   scale(semi_nat_prop) + 
                    (1 | farm), ziformula=~1, data=dung_beetle_q1_24)

# Model keeping the significant interactions of the global model. 
# Simplified model - AIC 210.1
  
summary(model3b)
##  Family: gaussian  ( identity )
## Formula:          
## shannon_log ~ scale(julian_day) + scale(woodland_prop) + scale(ag_prop) +  
##     scale(semi_nat_prop) + (1 | farm)
## Zero inflation:               ~1
## Data: dung_beetle_q1_24
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##    210.1    234.5    -97.0    194.1      148 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  farm     (Intercept) 0.002603 0.05102 
##  Residual             0.200690 0.44798 
## Number of obs: 156, groups:  farm, 18
## 
## Dispersion estimate for gaussian family (sigma^2): 0.201

# check_model(model3)

Simpson

# Look at log transformation.

dung_beetle_q2 <- dung_beetle %>% drop_na(q_2_simpson)
dung_beetle_q2$simpson_log <- log(dung_beetle_q2$q_2_simpson)

dung_beetle_q2_22 <- dung_beetle_q2 %>% filter(year == '2022')
dung_beetle_q2_24 <- dung_beetle_q2 %>% filter(year == '2024')

plot(fitdist(dung_beetle_q2$simpson_log,"norm"))

2022

# Model Simpson index vs. day of year, prop woodland, prop ag, prop semi-natural (and all two-way interactions). Farm as random variable. 

# Zero inflated model.

model4a <- glmmTMB(simpson_log ~ (scale(julian_day) + 
                                    scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                    scale(ag_prop) + 
                                    scale(semi_nat_prop))^2 + 
                     (1 | farm), ziformula=~1, data=dung_beetle_q2_22)

# Full model - AIC 197.7
# Significant interactions - Julian*woodland, Julian*semi-nat

model4a <- glmmTMB(simpson_log ~ scale(julian_day) + 
                                   scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                   scale(ag_prop) + 
                                   scale(semi_nat_prop) + 
                     scale(julian_day)*scale(semi_nat_prop) +
                     scale(julian_day)*scale(woodland_prop) +
                    (1 | farm), ziformula=~1, data=dung_beetle_q2_22)

# Model keeping the significant interactions of the global model. 
# Simplified model - AIC 195.2

summary(model4a)
##  Family: gaussian  ( identity )
## Formula:          
## simpson_log ~ scale(julian_day) + scale(woodland_prop) + scale(ag_prop) +  
##     scale(semi_nat_prop) + scale(julian_day) * scale(semi_nat_prop) +  
##     scale(julian_day) * scale(woodland_prop) + (1 | farm)
## Zero inflation:               ~1
## Data: dung_beetle_q2_22
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##    195.2    227.5    -87.6    175.2      176 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  farm     (Intercept) 0.008855 0.0941  
##  Residual             0.144174 0.3797  
## Number of obs: 186, groups:  farm, 10
## 
## Dispersion estimate for gaussian family (sigma^2): 0.144 
## 
## Conditional model:
##                                        Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                             0.45787    0.04085  11.207  < 2e-16 ***
## scale(julian_day)                       0.03099    0.02832   1.094  0.27375    
## scale(woodland_prop)                    0.07191    0.09509   0.756  0.44950

## scale(semi_nat_prop)                   -0.04992    0.05677  -0.879  0.37921    
## scale(julian_day):scale(semi_nat_prop)  0.03800    0.02992   1.270  0.20412    
## scale(julian_day):scale(woodland_prop)  0.08266    0.03017   2.740  0.00615 **

## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Native species abundance

plot(fitdist(dung_beetle$native_species_abundance,"pois"))

2022

# Model native species abundance vs. day of year, prop woodland, prop ag, prop semi-natural (and all two-way interactions). Farm as random variable. 

model5a <- glmer.nb(native_species_abundance ~ (scale(julian_day) + 
                                                  scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                                  scale(ag_prop) + 
                                                  scale(semi_nat_prop))^2 + 
                      (1 | farm), data = dung_beetle_22)
## Warning in checkConv(attr(opt, "derivs"), opt$par, ctrl = control$checkConv, :
## Model failed to converge with max|grad| = 0.00847791 (tol = 0.002, component 1)
# Full model - AIC 536.3
# Significant interactions - Julian*woodland, Julian*semi-nat

model5a <- glmer.nb(native_species_abundance ~ scale(julian_day) + 
                                   scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                   scale(ag_prop) + 
                                   scale(semi_nat_prop) +
                     scale(julian_day)*scale(semi_nat_prop) +
                     scale(julian_day)*scale(woodland_prop) +
                    (1 | farm), data=dung_beetle_22)

# Model keeping the significant interactions of the global model. 
# Simplified model - AIC 530.4
# When I tried to use season here as an interaction term with the landscape variables, the significant interactions were lost. 

summary(model5a)
## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]
##  Family: Negative Binomial(0.4549)  ( log )
## Formula: native_species_abundance ~ scale(julian_day) + scale(woodland_prop) +  
##     scale(ag_prop) + scale(semi_nat_prop) + scale(julian_day) *  
##     scale(semi_nat_prop) + scale(julian_day) * scale(woodland_prop) +  
##     (1 | farm)
##    Data: dung_beetle_22
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##    530.4    560.6   -256.2    512.4      203 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -0.6495 -0.4967 -0.4241 -0.0182  6.7013 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  farm   (Intercept) 0.3649   0.604   
## Number of obs: 212, groups:  farm, 10
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                        Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                            -0.48264    0.24701  -1.954 0.050713 .  
## scale(julian_day)                       0.02049    0.14966   0.137 0.891079    
## scale(woodland_prop)                   -0.23409    0.53850  -0.435 0.663770    
## scale(ag_prop)                         -0.34785    0.49359  -0.705 0.480972    
## scale(semi_nat_prop)                   -0.81963    0.33692  -2.433 0.014986 *  
## scale(julian_day):scale(semi_nat_prop)  0.43011    0.16452   2.614 0.008942 ** 
## scale(julian_day):scale(woodland_prop)  0.56049    0.15747   3.559 0.000372 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) scl(j_) scl(w_) scl(g_) sc(__) s(_):(__
## scl(jln_dy) -0.003                                        
## scl(wdlnd_)  0.035 -0.078                                 
## scal(g_prp)  0.034 -0.042   0.872                         
## scl(sm_nt_)  0.105 -0.063   0.694   0.607                 
## scl(_):(__) -0.102  0.141   0.007  -0.092  -0.027         
## scl(j_):(_) -0.107  0.133   0.028  -0.040   0.133  0.329
# check_model(model5)

2024

# Model native species abundance vs. day of year, prop woodland, prop ag, prop semi-natural (and all two-way interactions). Farm as random variable. 

model5b <- glmer.nb(native_species_abundance ~ (scale(julian_day) + 
                                                  scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                                  scale(ag_prop) + 
                                                  scale(semi_nat_prop))^2 + 
                      (1 | farm), data = dung_beetle_24)
## Warning in checkConv(attr(opt, "derivs"), opt$par, ctrl = control$checkConv, :
## Model failed to converge with max|grad| = 0.0269285 (tol = 0.002, component 1)
## Warning in checkConv(attr(opt, "derivs"), opt$par, ctrl = control$checkConv, :
## Model failed to converge with max|grad| = 0.00890668 (tol = 0.002, component 1)
# Full model - AIC 695.8
# Significant interactions - woodland*ag

model5b <- glmer.nb(native_species_abundance ~ scale(julian_day) + 
                                   scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                   scale(ag_prop) + 
                                   scale(semi_nat_prop) +
                     scale(ag_prop)*scale(woodland_prop) +
                    (1 | farm), data=dung_beetle_24)

# Model keeping the significant interactions of the global model. 
# Simplified model - AIC 692.4

summary(model5b)
## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]
##  Family: Negative Binomial(0.6553)  ( log )
## Formula: native_species_abundance ~ scale(julian_day) + scale(woodland_prop) +  
##     scale(ag_prop) + scale(semi_nat_prop) + scale(ag_prop) *

##    692.4    717.1   -338.2    676.4      155 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -0.7905 -0.6044 -0.4228  0.1317  4.7287

Native species richness

plot(fitdist(dung_beetle$native_species_richness,"pois"))

2022

# Model native species richness vs. day of year, prop woodland, prop ag, prop semi-natural (and all two-way interactions). Farm as random variable. 

model6a <- glmer(native_species_richness ~ (scale(julian_day) + 
                                             scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                             scale(ag_prop) + 
                                             scale(semi_nat_prop))^2 + 
                  (1 | farm), data = dung_beetle_22, family = poisson)

                                             scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                             scale(ag_prop) + 
                                             scale(semi_nat_prop) + 
                  (1 | farm), data = dung_beetle_22, family = poisson)

Native species proportion

2022

# Model native species proportion vs. day of year, prop woodland, prop ag, prop semi-natural (and all two-way interactions). Farm as random variable. 

model7a <- glmmTMB(native_species_proportion ~ (scale(julian_day) + 
                                                  scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                                  scale(ag_prop) + 
                                                  scale(semi_nat_prop))^2 + 
                     (1 | farm), data = dung_beetle_22, family=ordbeta)

# Full model - AIC 207.6
# No significant interactions

model7a <- glmmTMB(native_species_proportion ~ scale(julian_day) + 
                                                  scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                                  scale(ag_prop) + 
                                                  scale(semi_nat_prop) + 
                     (1 | farm), data = dung_beetle_22, family=ordbeta)

# Model keeping the significant interactions of the global model. 
# Simplified model - AIC 201.2

summary(model7a)
##  Family: ordbeta  ( logit )
## Formula:          
## native_species_proportion ~ scale(julian_day) + scale(woodland_prop) +  
##     scale(ag_prop) + scale(semi_nat_prop) + (1 | farm)
## Data: dung_beetle_22
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##    201.2    229.9    -91.6    183.2      170 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups Name        Variance  Std.Dev. 
##  farm   (Intercept) 3.072e-10 1.753e-05
## Number of obs: 179, groups:  farm, 10
## 
## Dispersion parameter for ordbeta family ():  5.1 
## 
## Conditional model:
##                      Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)           -1.4779     0.1347 -10.976  < 2e-16 ***
## scale(julian_day)      0.3837     0.1026   3.738 0.000185 ***
## scale(woodland_prop)  -0.3299     0.2056  -1.604 0.108671    
## scale(ag_prop)        -0.3390     0.1899  -1.785 0.074271 .  
## scale(semi_nat_prop)  -0.2362     0.1380  -1.712 0.086874 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# check_model(model7)

2024

# Model native species proportion vs. day of year, prop woodland, prop ag, prop semi-natural (and all two-way interactions). Farm as random variable. 

model7b <- glmmTMB(native_species_proportion ~ (scale(julian_day) + 
                                                  scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                                  scale(ag_prop) + 
                                                  scale(semi_nat_prop))^2 + 
                     (1 | farm), data = dung_beetle_24, family=ordbeta)

# Full model - AIC 68.6
# No significant interactions

model7b <- glmmTMB(native_species_proportion ~ scale(julian_day) + 
                                                  scale(woodland_prop) + 
                                                  scale(ag_prop) + 
                                                  scale(semi_nat_prop) + 
                     (1 | farm), data = dung_beetle_24, family=ordbeta)

# Model keeping the significant interactions of the global model. 
# Simplified model - AIC 60.9

summary(model7b)
